query: How do I switch to a different bachelor programme, and when does the change take effect?

queries: How do I switch to a different bachelor programme, and when does the change take effect?

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
retrieved.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

retrieved.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
retrieved.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text: #### Teacher Education programme: Change of a teaching subject This kind of change is not possible via u:space. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the [deadline](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/) (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). The Admission Office will effectuate the change within the admissio period, which you will see after a few days in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Further bachelor/diploma programmes
Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: Resuming the same bachelor/diploma programme
Bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text: resuming the same bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure - Bachelor programme in Psychology To resume your studies in the bachelor programme in Psychology, you have to successfully participate in the entrance exam procedure again. Apply for admission in u:space within the [application period for bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure](/aufnahmeverfahren/fristen-fuer-aufnahme-eignungsverfahren/). - [Detailed information about the entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Psychology](/en/entrance-exam/psychology/) - Bachelor programme in Teacher Education You can resume your studies with the same teaching subjects. If you want to change the teaching subject it is a change of degree programme. The same applies for the complementary teaching subject ("Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach"). For a change of degree programme you have to adhere to a different deadline! - [Detailed info about changing a teaching subject](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/#c806589) - Bachelor programme in Sport and Human Movement Science/Teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you have interrupted the bachelor programme in Sport and Human Movement Science or the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education for more than four semesters, you have to retake [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/). Please inform yourself regarding this subject at the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/). If you have to retake the admission test, you have to apply for admission in u:space within the [application period of the bachelor programme in Sport Science](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). The sporting aptitude admission test takes place twice a year. - Bachelor programme in Transcultural

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Further bachelor/diploma programmes
EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. Check if all study achievements from the degree programme you want to close are entered in your record of examinations in u:space. Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the new degree programme is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March).

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor programmes with compulsory OSA
EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship
retrieved.text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](https://self-assessment.univie.ac.at/) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. Check if all study achievements from the degree programme you want to close are entered in your record of examinations in u:space. Afterwards, message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) to close this degree programme. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the new degree programme is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March).

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: T
retrieved.text: period usually equals the length of the standard period of the curriculum. The transfer to the new curriculum comes into effect automatically at the end of this period. Information about the transfer is available in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your student record sheet (u:space > Personal matters > My documents). Application for transfer: You have to apply for transfer to a new curriculum at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme, provided that the transition period has not ended yet and both curricula are offered simultaneously. Recognition of exams: If your curriuclum does not provide any regulation on the recognition, your exams will be automatically recognised from the old curriculum to the new one after the admission . Otherwise, you must apply for the recognition of exams. For more information and advice, contact the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) in charge of your curriculum. Please note: Previously, inform yourself about possible legal consequences at your StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). It is not possible to reverse the transfer! Related Links: - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Translation Before you can apply for admission to a degree programme, you must have the required documents translated - unless they are issued in German or English. This also includes any legalisations. You should therefore first have the documents legalised (if required) and then translated into German by sworn

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: How to change your degree programme
retrieved.text: Use the selection below. There you will find instructions on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure. [### with compulsory OSA](#c806569) / - List of degree programmes A compulsory OSA is prescribed in the following bachelor programmes: - Astronomy - Comparative Literature - German Philology - Geography - History - Japanology - Mathematics - Oriental Studies - Philosophy - Physics - Romance Studies - Sinology - Slavonic Studies - Statistics and Data Analytics - Theatre, Film and Media Studies [### with entrance exam procedure](#c806577) / - List of degree programmes Entrance exam procedures are conducted in the following bachelor/diploma programmes: - Biology - Business Administration - Business Informatics - Chemistry - Computer Science - Economics - Education - English and American Studies - International Business Administration - International Legal Studies - Korean Studies - Law - Mass Media and Communication Science - Mathematical Foundations of Data Science - Nutritional Sciences - Pharmacy - Political Science - Psychology - Social and Cultural Anthropology - Sociology - Teacher Education - Transcultural Communication - Sport and Human Movement Science (sporting aptitude admission test) [### further bachelor/ diploma programmes](#c806593) / - List of degree programmes Neither a compulsory OSA, nor an entrance exam procedure are prescribed in the following bachelor/diploma programmes: - African Studies - Ancient History and Studies in Classical Antiquity - Byzantine Studies and Modern Greek Studies - Catholic

retrieved.title: Change of master programme
retrieved.text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c806701)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c806706)

Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). - Additional degree programme: Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) and tell us which programme you would like to study.

reranked.title: Change of degree programme
heading_path: General information regarding the change of a degree programme
reranked.text:
- Number of changes of degree programme: You can change your degree programme as often as you want to, according to the University of Vienna. However, a change of degree programme might impact your family allowance and/or study grant. Please contact the responsible authority (local Tax Office or Austrian Study Grant Authority) if you have any questions in this regard. Clarify the consequences on a potential residence permit with the Immigration Office (MA 35) or the responsible district commission ("Bezirkshauptmannschaft").
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 onwards you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed.
- Transferring to the University of Vienna from another university: The information regarding the change of degree programmes applies to students admitted at the University of Vienna. Students who wish to transfer to the University of Vienna have to complete the admission procedure. The entry into a higher semester is not possible. After [completing the admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) you can apply for potential recognition of your previous examinations at the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/search.html?filter=all&query=StudienServiceCenter) responsible for your degree programme. Thereby you might shorten the duration of your degree programme.

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
reranked.text: #### Teacher Education programme: Change of a teaching subject This kind of change is not possible via u:space. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the [deadline](/en/entrance-exam/exemptions-from-entrance-exam-procedure/) (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). The Admission Office will effectuate the change within the admissio period, which you will see after a few days in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Further bachelor/diploma programmes
Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
reranked.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

reranked.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: Resuming the same bachelor/diploma programme
Bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure
reranked.text: resuming the same bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure - Bachelor programme in Psychology To resume your studies in the bachelor programme in Psychology, you have to successfully participate in the entrance exam procedure again. Apply for admission in u:space within the [application period for bachelor programmes with entrance exam procedure](/aufnahmeverfahren/fristen-fuer-aufnahme-eignungsverfahren/). - [Detailed information about the entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Psychology](/en/entrance-exam/psychology/) - Bachelor programme in Teacher Education You can resume your studies with the same teaching subjects. If you want to change the teaching subject it is a change of degree programme. The same applies for the complementary teaching subject ("Erweiterungsstudium drittes Unterrichtsfach"). For a change of degree programme you have to adhere to a different deadline! - [Detailed info about changing a teaching subject](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/#c806589) - Bachelor programme in Sport and Human Movement Science/Teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you have interrupted the bachelor programme in Sport and Human Movement Science or the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education for more than four semesters, you have to retake [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/). Please inform yourself regarding this subject at the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/). If you have to retake the admission test, you have to apply for admission in u:space within the [application period of the bachelor programme in Sport Science](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). The sporting aptitude admission test takes place twice a year. - Bachelor programme in Transcultural

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: T
reranked.text: period usually equals the length of the standard period of the curriculum. The transfer to the new curriculum comes into effect automatically at the end of this period. Information about the transfer is available in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your student record sheet (u:space > Personal matters > My documents). Application for transfer: You have to apply for transfer to a new curriculum at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme, provided that the transition period has not ended yet and both curricula are offered simultaneously. Recognition of exams: If your curriuclum does not provide any regulation on the recognition, your exams will be automatically recognised from the old curriculum to the new one after the admission . Otherwise, you must apply for the recognition of exams. For more information and advice, contact the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) in charge of your curriculum. Please note: Previously, inform yourself about possible legal consequences at your StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). It is not possible to reverse the transfer! Related Links: - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Translation Before you can apply for admission to a degree programme, you must have the required documents translated - unless they are issued in German or English. This also includes any legalisations. You should therefore first have the documents legalised (if required) and then translated into German by sworn

reranked.text: Use the selection below. There you will find instructions on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure. [### with compulsory OSA](#c806569) / - List of degree programmes A compulsory OSA is prescribed in the following bachelor programmes: - Astronomy - Comparative Literature - German Philology - Geography - History - Japanology - Mathematics - Oriental Studies - Philosophy - Physics - Romance Studies - Sinology - Slavonic Studies - Statistics and Data Analytics - Theatre, Film and Media Studies [### with entrance exam procedure](#c806577) / - List of degree programmes Entrance exam procedures are conducted in the following bachelor/diploma programmes: - Biology - Business Administration - Business Informatics - Chemistry - Computer Science - Economics - Education - English and American Studies - International Business Administration - International Legal Studies - Korean Studies - Law - Mass Media and Communication Science - Mathematical Foundations of Data Science - Nutritional Sciences - Pharmacy - Political Science - Psychology - Social and Cultural Anthropology - Sociology - Teacher Education - Transcultural Communication - Sport and Human Movement Science (sporting aptitude admission test) [### further bachelor/ diploma programmes](#c806593) / - List of degree programmes Neither a compulsory OSA, nor an entrance exam procedure are prescribed in the following bachelor/diploma programmes: - African Studies - Ancient History and Studies in Classical Antiquity - Byzantine Studies and Modern Greek Studies - Catholic

reranked.title: Change of master programme
reranked.text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c806701)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c806706)

selected.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
selected.text:
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

selected.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
selected.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

selected.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: FAQ
selected.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

heading_path: Changing your degree programme or studying an additional programme within certain groups

You can change between these three programmes or study two or all three programmes. selected.title: Change of degree programme
selected.text:
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-master-programme/)

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
You would like to change your bachelor/diploma programme?

Your steps in the change of degree programme depend on two factors:

1. Does your chosen degree programme have a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure?
2. Is your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country?

[2] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure > Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
- Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the

[3] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — FAQ
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

[4] Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure — Changing your degree programme or studying an additional programme within certain groups > Studiengruppen
Collapse all Open all #### Business Administration ← → International Business Administration You are admitted to one of these two bachelor's programmes at the University of Vienna and want to change to the other programme or study both? Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). - Additional degree programme: Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) and tell us which programme you would like to study. #### Computer Science ← → Business Informatics You are admitted to one of these two bachelor's programmes at the University of Vienna and want to change to the other programme or study both? Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which you want to study in the future in the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). - Additional degree programme: Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) and tell us which programme you would like to study. #### Political Science ← → Sociology ← → Cultural- and Social Anthropology You are admitted to one of these three bachelor's programmes at the University of Vienna? You can change between these three programmes or study two or all three programmes. Note the [deadline](#c4319) and message us. - Changing your degree programme: Define which programme you want to close and which

[5] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure > Special cases when changing a bachelor/diploma programme
aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

[6] Change of degree programme
You are currently studying at the University of Vienna and would like to change your degree programme? Your steps in the admission procedure and the application period depend on the type of degree programme.

[## Bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/)

[## Master programmes](/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-master-programme/)

Question: How do I switch to a different bachelor programme, and when does the change take effect?

generate.raw_response: To switch to a different bachelor programme, you need to follow steps that depend on two factors: whether your chosen degree programme has a compulsory Online-Self-Assessment (OSA) or entrance exam procedure, and whether your secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship are from an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or from a non-EU/EEA country [1]. You should submit your application for admission within the application period [3] and send a message via the Servicedesk indicating the desired change [2][4][5]. The change takes effect starting from the next semester, and you will see the change in u:space after a few days and receive a confirmation via e-mail [2].

You should submit your application for admission within the application period [3] and send a message via the Servicedesk indicating the desired change [2][4][5]. The change takes effect starting from the next semester, and you will see the change in u:space after a few days and receive a confirmation via e-mail [2].
